title: Interpreters and Translators
description: Interpret oral or sign language, or translate written text from one language into another.

tasks.text: Follow ethical codes that protect the confidentiality of information.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Translate messages simultaneously or consecutively into specified languages, orally or by using hand signs, maintaining message content, context, and style as much as possible.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Listen to speakers' statements to determine meanings and to prepare translations, using electronic listening systems as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Compile terminology and information to be used in translations, including technical terms such as those for legal or medical material.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Refer to reference materials, such as dictionaries, lexicons, encyclopedias, and computerized terminology banks, as needed to ensure translation accuracy.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check translations of technical terms and terminology to ensure that they are accurate and remain consistent throughout translation revisions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Identify and resolve conflicts related to the meanings of words, concepts, practices, or behaviors.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Compile information on content and context of information to be translated and on intended audience.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Adapt translations to students' cognitive and grade levels, collaborating with educational team members as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check original texts or confer with authors to ensure that translations retain the content, meaning, and feeling of the original material.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Adapt software and accompanying technical documents to another language and culture.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Educate students, parents, staff, and teachers about the roles and functions of educational interpreters.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Proofread, edit, and revise translated materials.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Train and supervise other translators or interpreters.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Read written materials, such as legal documents, scientific works, or news reports, and rewrite material into specified languages.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Travel with or guide tourists who speak another language.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Discuss translation requirements with clients and determine any fees to be charged for services provided.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
